interfaces.description:
Criteria for filtering a parsed game collection in the PGN processor worker.

All string fields are matched case-insensitively as substrings. Rating fields default to 0 (no lower bound) or Infinity (no upper bound) when unset.

const criteria: FilterCriteria = {
  white: 'carlsen',
  black: '',
  result: '1-0',
  moves: true,
  ignoreColor: false,
  targetMoves: ['e4', 'e5', 'Nf3'],
  minWhiteRating: 2500,
  minBlackRating: 2400,
  maxWhiteRating: Infinity,
  maxBlackRating: Infinity,
  eco: 'B33',
  timeControl: '180+2',
  event: '',
};
interfaces.rawdescription:
Criteria for filtering a parsed game collection in the PGN processor worker.

All string fields are matched case-insensitively as substrings.
Rating fields default to 0 (no lower bound) or Infinity (no upper bound) when unset.

```typescript
  white: 'carlsen',

  minWhiteRating: 2500,
  minBlackRating: 2400,
  maxWhiteRating: Infinity,
  maxBlackRating: Infinity,
  eco: 'B33',
  timeControl: '180+2',
  event: '',
};
```

injectables.description:
Service that manages Web Workers for background PGN processing and Stockfish analysis.

Maintains two workers:

  • PGN processor — parses/filters PGN data off the main thread using pgn-processor.worker.
  • Stockfish — runs the Stockfish chess engine for position analysis via UCI protocol.

Provided at root level so a single instance is shared across the application. Callers must call {@link initialize} before using the service and {@link dispose} when done.
injectables.rawdescription:
Service that manages Web Workers for background PGN processing and Stockfish analysis.

Maintains two workers:
- **PGN processor** — parses/filters PGN data off the main thread using `pgn-processor.worker`.
- **Stockfish** — runs the Stockfish chess engine for position analysis via UCI protocol.

Provided at root level so a single instance is shared across the application.
Callers must call {@link initialize} before using the service and {@link dispose} when done.

components.description:
Core chessboard component wrapping the chessground library via snabbdom.

Accepts a runFunction signal-model input that receives the mounted DOM element and must return a chessground Api instance. The component manages lifecycle:

  • Uses an Angular effect() to watch runFunction changes and redraw the board.
  • Calls redraw() on ngAfterViewInit so the board appears as soon as the view is ready.
  • Provides a toggleOrientation() method to flip the board.

Uses {@link NgxChessgroundService} (provided at component level) for snabbdom patching and chessground instance management.

<ngx-chessground [runFunction]="myRunFn()" />
myRunFn = signal<(el: HTMLElement) => Api>((el) => {
  return Chessground(el, { fen: 'start' });
});
components.rawdescription:
Core chessboard component wrapping the chessground library via snabbdom.

Accepts a `runFunction` signal-model input that receives the mounted DOM element
and must return a chessground `Api` instance. The component manages lifecycle:
- Uses an Angular `effect()` to watch `runFunction` changes and redraw the board.
- Calls `redraw()` on `ngAfterViewInit` so the board appears as soon as the view is ready.
- Provides a `toggleOrientation()` method to flip the board.

Uses {@link NgxChessgroundService} (provided at component level) for snabbdom patching
and chessground instance management.

```html
<ngx-chessground [runFunction]="myRunFn()" />
```

```typescript
myRunFn = signal<(el: HTMLElement) => Api>((el) => {
  return Chessground(el, { fen: 'start' });
});
```

components.description:
A full-featured PGN viewer component for Angular applications.

Supports loading single or multi-game PGN files, navigating moves, auto-replay with configurable timing modes, Stockfish-powered position analysis ("stop on error"), filtering by player/ECO/opening moves, and batch replay across multiple games.

All components used are standalone. Import this component directly:

imports: [NgxPgnViewerComponent]
<ngx-pgn-viewer [pgn]="pgnString" [highlightLastMove]="true" />
components.rawdescription:
A full-featured PGN viewer component for Angular applications.

Supports loading single or multi-game PGN files, navigating moves, auto-replay
with configurable timing modes, Stockfish-powered position analysis ("stop on error"),
filtering by player/ECO/opening moves, and batch replay across multiple games.

All components used are standalone. Import this component directly:
```typescript
imports: [NgxPgnViewerComponent]
```

```html
<ngx-pgn-viewer [pgn]="pgnString" [highlightLastMove]="true" />
```

constructorObj.name: constructor
constructorObj.description:
Initializes the PGN viewer engine workers and sets up reactive effects.

Three effects are registered:

  1. Auto-updates the Lichess URL when year/month selection changes.
  2. Loads the initial PGN from the bound input when provided.
  3. Auto-scrolls the move list when the current move index changes.

variables.name: ECO_MOVES
variables.rawdescription:
Mapping from ECO (Encyclopedia of Chess Openings) codes to their defining move sequences.

Each entry maps an ECO code (e.g. `'A00'`, `'B33'`) to the pipe-separated sequence of
standard algebraic notation (SAN) moves that identify the opening.

Generated from a curated ECO database. Used by the PGN viewer for opening classification
and filtering by specific opening lines.

| Category | ECO Range |
|----------|-----------|
| A        | Flank openings (A00–A99) |
| B        | Semi-open games (B00–B99) |
| C        | Open games / French (C00–C99) |
| D        | Closed / Semi-closed games (D00–D99) |
| E        | Indian defenses (E00–E99) |

variables.name: pgn
variables.rawdescription:
A PGN (Portable Game Notation) string representing a chess game.

The PGN includes metadata about the game such as event, site, date, players,
their ratings, and the result. It also contains the moves of the game along
with timestamps for each move.

Example metadata:
- Event: Rated Blitz game
- Site: https://lichess.org/hvB20kxq
- Date: 2018.05.19
- White: topce
- Black: donateIIo
- Result: 1-0
- WhiteElo: 2342
- BlackElo: 2406
- WhiteRatingDiff: +12
- BlackRatingDiff: -12
- BlackTitle: GM
- Variant: Standard
- TimeControl: 180+0
- ECO: A00
- Opening: Anderssen Opening
- Termination: Normal

Example moves:
1. a3 { [%clk 0:03:00] } a6 { [%clk 0:03:00] }
2. b3 { [%clk 0:02:59] } h6 { [%clk 0:03:00] }
3. c3 { [%clk 0:02:59] } Nf6 { [%clk 0:02:57] }
...
39. Rd7# { [%clk 0:00:55] } 1-0

functions.name: extractGameInfo
functions.description:
Extracts metadata from a single game's PGN header tags.

Parses White, Black, Result, WhiteElo, BlackElo, WhiteTitle, BlackTitle, ECO, TimeControl, and Event tags. Formats player names with title+Elo (e.g. "GM Carlsen (2850)"). Normalizes the time control field.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string for a single game.
— Zero-based game index (result's number will be index + 1).
Populated {@link GameMetadata} object.

functions.name: extractMovesFast
functions.description:
Extracts SAN moves from a PGN string without using a full chess engine parser.

Used for filtering by opening moves — faster than full chess.js parsing. Steps:

  1. Isolates move text after header tags, handling bracket comments like [%clk ...].
  2. Strips { ... } and ( ... ) comments and ; ... line comments.
  3. Removes move numbers, result markers, and NAG symbols ($1, $2).
  4. Splits by whitespace, strips !/? annotations, and validates SAN format.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string for a single game (header tags + move text).
Array of clean, validated SAN move strings.

functions.name: handleFilter
functions.description:
Filters the currently loaded game collection by the given criteria.

Matches are case-insensitive substring comparisons. When moves is true, tests whether each game's opening sequence starts with targetMoves. Posts a 'filter' response with an array of matching game indices.

jsdoctags.comment: — Filter parameters (player names, ECO, result, ratings, opening moves).
— Correlation ID echoed in the response.

functions.name: handleLoad
functions.description: Loads raw PGN text: splits into individual games, filters out non-Standard variants, extracts metadata, and posts a 'load' response with game count and metadata.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string potentially containing multiple games.
— Correlation ID echoed in the response.

functions.name: handleLoadGame
functions.description:
Parses the full move data for a single game by index.

Uses chess.js for primary parsing with fallback to chessops and stripped-comment parsing. Extracts move evaluations from [%eval ...] comment annotations when available. Results are cached in {@link gameMovesCache} to avoid re-parsing. Posts a 'loadGame' response with moves, cleaned PGN, evaluations, and optional error.

jsdoctags.comment: — Zero-based game index in the games array.
— Correlation ID echoed in the response.

functions.name: normalizeResult
functions.description:
Normalizes PGN result strings into a canonical form.

  • "1-0" → "1-0" (white win)
  • "0-1" → "0-1" (black win)
  • "½-½", "1/2-1/2" → "draw"
  • Unknown/unparseable → lowercased as-is

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw result string from a PGN [Result "..."] header.
Canonical result string.

functions.name: normalizeTimeControl
functions.description:
Normalizes a PGN [TimeControl "..."] value into a uniform "baseSeconds+incrementSeconds" format.

Handles formats including:

  • "h:m:s" (e.g. "1:30:0" → "5400+0")
  • "m:s" (e.g. "90+30" → "5400+30")
  • "base+inc" (e.g. "180+2" → "180+2")
  • Textual: "90 min + 30 sec", "3'", "3 min", etc.

Heuristic: base values ≤ 180 are treated as minutes unless a min/mins/' suffix is present.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw time control string from the PGN header, or undefined.
Normalized string like "5400+30", or undefined if unparseable.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: ECO_MOVES
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.rawdescription:
Mapping from ECO (Encyclopedia of Chess Openings) codes to their defining move sequences.

Each entry maps an ECO code (e.g. `'A00'`, `'B33'`) to the pipe-separated sequence of
standard algebraic notation (SAN) moves that identify the opening.

Generated from a curated ECO database. Used by the PGN viewer for opening classification
and filtering by specific opening lines.

| Category | ECO Range |
|----------|-----------|
| A        | Flank openings (A00–A99) |
| B        | Semi-open games (B00–B99) |
| C        | Open games / French (C00–C99) |
| D        | Closed / Semi-closed games (D00–D99) |
| E        | Indian defenses (E00–E99) |

projects/ngx-chessground/src/units/pgn.t.name: pgn
projects/ngx-chessground/src/units/pgn.t.rawdescription:
A PGN (Portable Game Notation) string representing a chess game.

The PGN includes metadata about the game such as event, site, date, players,
their ratings, and the result. It also contains the moves of the game along
with timestamps for each move.

Example metadata:
- Event: Rated Blitz game
- Site: https://lichess.org/hvB20kxq
- Date: 2018.05.19
- White: topce
- Black: donateIIo
- Result: 1-0
- WhiteElo: 2342
- BlackElo: 2406
- WhiteRatingDiff: +12
- BlackRatingDiff: -12
- BlackTitle: GM
- Variant: Standard
- TimeControl: 180+0
- ECO: A00
- Opening: Anderssen Opening
- Termination: Normal

Example moves:
1. a3 { [%clk 0:03:00] } a6 { [%clk 0:03:00] }
2. b3 { [%clk 0:02:59] } h6 { [%clk 0:03:00] }
3. c3 { [%clk 0:02:59] } Nf6 { [%clk 0:02:57] }
...
39. Rd7# { [%clk 0:00:55] } 1-0
projects/ngx-chessground/src/units/pgn.t.description:
A PGN (Portable Game Notation) string representing a chess game.

The PGN includes metadata about the game such as event, site, date, players, their ratings, and the result. It also contains the moves of the game along with timestamps for each move.

Example metadata:

  • Event: Rated Blitz game
  • Site: https://lichess.org/hvB20kxq
  • Date: 2018.05.19
  • White: topce
  • Black: donateIIo
  • Result: 1-0
  • WhiteElo: 2342
  • BlackElo: 2406
  • WhiteRatingDiff: +12
  • BlackRatingDiff: -12
  • BlackTitle: GM
  • Variant: Standard
  • TimeControl: 180+0
  • ECO: A00
  • Opening: Anderssen Opening
  • Termination: Normal

Example moves:

  1. a3 { [%clk 0:03:00] } a6 { [%clk 0:03:00] }
  2. b3 { [%clk 0:02:59] } h6 { [%clk 0:03:00] }
  3. c3 { [%clk 0:02:59] } Nf6 { [%clk 0:02:57] } ...
  4. Rd7# { [%clk 0:00:55] } 1-0

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: extractGameInfo
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Extracts metadata from a single game's PGN header tags.

Parses White, Black, Result, WhiteElo, BlackElo, WhiteTitle, BlackTitle, ECO, TimeControl, and Event tags. Formats player names with title+Elo (e.g. "GM Carlsen (2850)"). Normalizes the time control field.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string for a single game.
— Zero-based game index (result's number will be index + 1).
Populated {@link GameMetadata} object.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: extractMovesFast
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Extracts SAN moves from a PGN string without using a full chess engine parser.

Used for filtering by opening moves — faster than full chess.js parsing. Steps:

  1. Isolates move text after header tags, handling bracket comments like [%clk ...].
  2. Strips { ... } and ( ... ) comments and ; ... line comments.
  3. Removes move numbers, result markers, and NAG symbols ($1, $2).
  4. Splits by whitespace, strips !/? annotations, and validates SAN format.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string for a single game (header tags + move text).
Array of clean, validated SAN move strings.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: handleFilter
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Filters the currently loaded game collection by the given criteria.

Matches are case-insensitive substring comparisons. When moves is true, tests whether each game's opening sequence starts with targetMoves. Posts a 'filter' response with an array of matching game indices.

jsdoctags.comment: — Filter parameters (player names, ECO, result, ratings, opening moves).
— Correlation ID echoed in the response.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: handleLoad
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description: Loads raw PGN text: splits into individual games, filters out non-Standard variants, extracts metadata, and posts a 'load' response with game count and metadata.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string potentially containing multiple games.
— Correlation ID echoed in the response.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: handleLoadGame
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Parses the full move data for a single game by index.

Uses chess.js for primary parsing with fallback to chessops and stripped-comment parsing. Extracts move evaluations from [%eval ...] comment annotations when available. Results are cached in {@link gameMovesCache} to avoid re-parsing. Posts a 'loadGame' response with moves, cleaned PGN, evaluations, and optional error.

jsdoctags.comment: — Zero-based game index in the games array.
— Correlation ID echoed in the response.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: normalizeResult
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Normalizes PGN result strings into a canonical form.

  • "1-0" → "1-0" (white win)
  • "0-1" → "0-1" (black win)
  • "½-½", "1/2-1/2" → "draw"
  • Unknown/unparseable → lowercased as-is

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw result string from a PGN [Result "..."] header.
Canonical result string.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: normalizeTimeControl
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Normalizes a PGN [TimeControl "..."] value into a uniform "baseSeconds+incrementSeconds" format.

Handles formats including:

  • "h:m:s" (e.g. "1:30:0" → "5400+0")
  • "m:s" (e.g. "90+30" → "5400+30")
  • "base+inc" (e.g. "180+2" → "180+2")
  • Textual: "90 min + 30 sec", "3'", "3 min", etc.

Heuristic: base values ≤ 180 are treated as minutes unless a min/mins/' suffix is present.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw time control string from the PGN header, or undefined.
Normalized string like "5400+30", or undefined if unparseable.

projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.name: splitPgn
projects/ngx-chessground/src/lib/pgn-vie.description:
Splits a multi-game PGN string into individual game strings.

Splits on [Event "..." tag boundaries, which marks the start of each game. Handles concatenated games where the tag may appear anywhere in the text.

jsdoctags.comment: — Raw PGN string containing one or more games.
Array of individual game PGN strings.
